title: Chronik to Heimlern outcome bridge
goal: Make operator routing outcomes available to Heimlern through a Chronik-owned append-only envelope, prove on a bounded real sample whether the result improves an operator diagnosis or decision, and keep all analysis proposal-only.
completion: Chronik transport and export surfaces and runtime state are audited before implementation claims.
A redacted Chronik envelope binds the Heimlern-owned payload revision and digests without duplicating payload authority.
Heimlern consumes the envelope through a dedicated typed, review-only path and emits a report, valid proposal or insufficient-evidence result.
Grabowski has a separately reviewed, opt-in producer and a bounded real-outcome proof records whether Heimlern materially improves an operator diagnosis or decision.
Only a positive usefulness result may promote Bureau proposal-review workflow or an optional Leitstand read-only projection.

metadata.source_decision: Chronik is the primary historical transport organ; Heimlern is the routing-outcome payload and offline-analysis authority.
metadata.audit_finding: The transport envelope and dedicated typed Heimlern consumer are implemented. The next missing evidence is not another display contract but a real, redacted Grabowski sample and an explicit operator-value verdict.
metadata.sequencing_decision: T005 real-outcome usefulness precedes T004 Bureau or Leitstand product surfaces.
